km.Renfrewshire, Scotland: រេនហ្វ្រែwស៊ីរe, ស្កុតឡេន

zh.lifeSummary: Robertus de Pollock II是Baron Robertus De Pollock I的兒子，於1119年出生在蘇格蘭雷福福爾。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1203年在他的家鄉享年84歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Robertus de Pollock II是Baron Robertus De Pollock I的儿子，于1119年出生在苏格兰雷福福爾。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1203年在他的家乡享年84岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pollock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from a place in Glasgow, probably a British Celtic name derived from pull-, poll- ‘pool’ + a diminutive suffix. The surname has been established in northeast Ulster since the 17th century.
German: ethnic name for someone from Poland.
Americanized form of Jewish   Polak  .

variants.name: Pogue
meanings: Irish (Cavan): shortened form of the Scottish name Pollock 1, Gaelicized as Polóc, later assimilated to the Irish word póg ‘kiss’.

variants.name: Pollick
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Pollock  . This surname is rare in Britain.
Americanized form of Slovenian Polič, Croatian and Serbian Polić (see Polich ). Compare Polick .

variants.name: Palk
meanings: English (Devon): shortened form of Pollock , with unrounding of the vowel to give Palk. Compare Polk and also Pulford . Derivation from a shortened form of the Middle English personal names Palcok and Palkin (pet forms of Paul) is formally possible, but no evidence has been found to substantiate this.

variants.name: Paulk
meanings: English (Devon) and Scottish: shortened form of   Pollock  . The Scottish name is a variant of   Polk  .
Altered form of a German surname, possibly   Paulke  .

variants.name: Polk
meanings: Scottish: shortened form of   Pollock  .
Sorbian (Półk and Pólk): probably from a short form of a personal name based on Old Slavic plъkъ ‘crowd, army’ (see Polka 3).
East German (of Slavic origin): variant of   Polke  .

variants.name: Pollok
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and Renfrewshire): variant of   Pollock  .

variants.name: Polak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Miroslaw, Zbigniew, Boguslawa, Dorota, Jacek, Andrzej, Bogdan, Eugeniusz, Grzegorz, Irena, Jerzy, Jozef.

Polish and Jewish (Ashkenazic); Czech, Slovak, and Hungarian (Polák, Slovak also Poľák): ethnic name for someone from Poland (Polish Polak, Slovak archaic and Czech Polák). In the case of the Ashkenazic name, the reference is to a Jew from Poland. This surname is also found in Germany, where it is also of Sorbian origin (see 3 below), and in the Netherlands. Compare Poliak , Poljak , and Polyak .

Slovenian and Croatian: topographic name for someone who lived in open country, derived from polje ‘field’, or a habitational name for someone from any of numerous places called Polje, named with this word. It may also be e.g. an occupational name for a field guard (see Poljak ).

nameMeanings.name: Robertus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the personal name Robert , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is also occasionally borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Robert
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

variants.name: Robers
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

variants.name: Roberds
meanings: English: variant of   Roberts  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Robards
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Roberts  .

variants.name: Robarts
meanings: English (Bedfordshire and Hertfortshire): variant of   Roberts  .

en.lifeSummary: Robertus de Pollock II was born in 1119, in Renfrewshire, Scotland as the son of Baron Robertus De Pollock I. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1203, in his hometown, at the age of 84.

fr.lifeSummary: Robertus de Pollock II est né en 1119, à Renfrewshire, Écosse, fils de Baron Robertus De Pollock I. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1203, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 84 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Robertus de Pollock II wurde 1119 in Renfrewshire, Scotland, als Sohn von Baron Robertus De Pollock I geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1203 im Alter von 84 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Robertus de Pollock II è nato il nel 1119 nel Renfrewshire, Scotland come figlio di Baron Robertus De Pollock I. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1203, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 84 anni.

ja.Renfrewshire, Scotland: スコットランドレンフリューシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Robertus de Pollock IIは1119年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアでBaron Robertus De Pollock Iの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1203年に故郷で84歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robertus de Pollock II(은)는 1119년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르에서 Baron Robertus De Pollock I의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1203년 그의 고향에서 84세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Robertus de Pollock II nasceu em 1119, em Renfrewshire, Scotland filho de Baron Robertus De Pollock I. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1203, em sua cidade natal, com 84 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Robertus de Pollock II родился в 1119 г. в местности ренфрюшир, Шотландия. Его отца звали Baron Robertus De Pollock I. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1203 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 84 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Robertus de Pollock II nació en 1119, en Renfrewshire, Scotland como hijo de Baron Robertus De Pollock I. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1203, en su pueblo, a la edad de 84 años.
